title: Logging In
content:
# Kuingia

Ingia kwenye akaunti yako, weka upya nenosiri ulilosahau, na utoke nje ukishamaliza.

## Jinsi ya Kuingia

1. Nenda kwenye **[ukurasa wa kuingia](/login)** kwa kubofya ikoni ya mtumiaji kwenye kona ya juu kulia na kuchagua **Ingia**
2. Weka **Barua pepe** na **Nenosiri** zako
3. Bofya **Ingia**

Ikiwa siri zako ni sahihi, utaelekezwa kwenye ukurasa wa kuchagua lugha (au kurudi kwenye ukurasa uliokuwa ukiondoka ulipoingia).

Ikiwa utaingiza barua pepe au nenosiri lisilo sahihi, ujumbe wa hitilafu utaonekana — kagua sehemu zote mbili na ujaribu tena.

## Huna Akaunti?

Bofya **Jiandikishe** chini ya fomu ya kuingia ili kuunda akaunti mpya. Tazama [Usajili](/docs/account/registration) kwa mwongozo kamili wa kujiandikisha.

## Umesahau Nenosiri Lako?

Ikiwa huwezi kukumbuka nenosiri lako:

1. Kwenye ukurasa wa kuingia, bofya **Umesahau nenosiri?**
2. Weka anwani yako ya barua pepe na bofya **Tuma kiungo cha kuweka upya**
3. Angalia kikasha chako cha barua pepe kwa ujumbe wa kuweka upya nenosiri
4. Fuata kiungo kilicho kwenye barua pepe ili kuweka nenosiri jipya

Kwa usalama, ukurasa huwa unaonyesha ujumbe wa mafanikio baada ya kuwasilisha — hata kama barua pepe haijasajiliwa. Hii inazuia mtu yeyote kuangalia kama barua pepe fulani ina akaunti.

> **Kumbuka:** Kiungo cha kuweka upya nenosiri kinatoka kwa Directus, mtoa huduma wetu wa utambulisho. Ikiwa huoni barua pepe ndani ya dakika chache, angalia folda yako ya spam.

## Jinsi ya Kutoka Nje

Bofya ikoni ya mtumiaji kwenye kona ya juu kulia (inaonyesha herufi yako ya kwanza), kisha bofya **Toka** chini ya menyu. Utatolewa nje na kuelekezwa kwenye ukurasa wa mwanzo.

Kutoka nje kunafuta kipindi chako cha ndani, lakini maneno yako yaliyohifadhiwa, historia ya kutazama, na mipangilio yako vimehifadhiwa — vitarejeshwa utakapoingia tena.

## Kubadilisha Akaunti

Ili kubadili akaunti tofauti, toka nje kwanza, kisha ingia kwa anwani nyingine ya barua pepe. Hakuna kibadilisha akaunti — kila kipindi cha kivinjari kinatumia akaunti moja kwa wakati mmoja.

## Vidokezo

- Kipindi chako cha kuingia kinaendelea hata ukianza upya kivinjari — hautahitaji kuingia kila wakati unapotembelea
- Ikiwa unatumia kifaa cha pamoja, toka nje kila wakati ukishamaliza
- Mchakato wa nenosiri lililosahaulika unafanya kazi hata kama ulijisajili kwa barua pepe sawa — inatuma kiungo cha kuweka upya bila kujali
- Unaweza kudhibiti maelezo ya akaunti yako kutoka kwenye **[Wasifu yako](/docs/account/profile)**

title: Wasifu
content:
# Wasifu

Ukurasa wako wa wasifu unaonyesha taarifa za akaunti, kiwango cha lugha, hali ya usajili, historia ya kutazama, na maneno yaliyohifadhiwa — yote mahali pamoja.

## Taarifa za Akaunti

Juu ya ukurasa utaona jina lako la kuonyesha na anwani ya barua pepe. Hii ndiyo taarifa inayohusishwa na akaunti yako — ndiyo uliyotumia ulipojisajili.

## Kiwango cha Lugha

Sehemu ya **Mipangilio** inakuwezesha kuweka kiwango chako cha ustadi kwa lugha lengwa ya sasa. Chagua kutoka kwenye orodha kunjuzi ili kuendana na uwezo wako:

- **Kichina** inaonyesha viwango vya HSK (1 hadi 7-9)
- **Kijapani** inaonyesha viwango vya JLPT (Pre-N5 hadi N1)
- **Kikorea** inaonyesha viwango vya TOPIK (Pre-1 hadi 6)
- **Kiingereza** inaonyesha viwango vya IELTS (1 hadi 9)
- **Lugha nyingine zote** zinaonyesha viwango vya CEFR (Pre-A1 hadi C2)

Kiwango chako kinatumika kuchuja mapendekezo ya video kwenye ukurasa wa [Chunguza](/docs/media/explore) na kuweka rangi kwenye beji za ugumu kwenye kadi za video. Unaweza kubadilisha wakati wowote — mapendekezo yako yanasasishwa mara moja.

## Usajili

Sehemu ya **Usajili** inaonyesha mpango wako wa sasa na inakuwezesha kuisimamia.

### Akaunti Bure

Ikiwa unatumia akaunti bure, utaona beji ya **Akaunti Bure** na kadi tatu za mipango — Kila Mwezi ($10/mwezi), Kila Mwaka ($90/mwaka), na Maisha Yote ($169). Bonyeza **Boresha hadi Pro** kwenda kwenye ukurasa wa [Boresha hadi Pro](/docs/account/billing) na uchague mpango.

### Usajili Amilifu wa Pro

Ikiwa una usajili amilifu, utaona:

- Beji ya **Kila Mwezi**, **Kila Mwaka**, au **Maisha**
- Siku zilizobaki hadi kufanywa upya (mipango ya kila mwezi na kila mwaka)
- Beji ya **Inajifanyia upya kiotomatiki** ikiwa kufanywa upya kiotomatiki kumewashwa
- Njia ya malipo iliyotumiwa (Stripe, PayPal, au Apple App Store)

Kutoka hapa unaweza:

- **Ghairi Urejeshaji Kiotomatiki** — inasimamisha kufanywa upya kiotomatiki mwishoni mwa kipindi chako cha sasa cha malipo. Upatikanaji wako wa Pro unabaki amilifu hadi wakati huo.
- **Boresha** — badilisha kutoka Kila Mwezi hadi Kila Mwaka, au kutoka mpango wowote hadi Maisha Yote
- **Badilisha hadhi ya Maisha Yote** — malipo ya mara moja ya $169 kwa upatikanaji wa kudumu wa Pro

### Usajili Uliokwisha

Ikiwa usajili wako umekwisha, utaona beji nyekundu ya kumalizika muda na kitufe cha **Sasisha**. Maneno yako yote yaliyohifadhiwa, historia ya kutazama, na mipangilio yote yanahifadhiwa — fanya upya tu ili kuendelea pale ulipoacha.

## Historia ya Kutazama

Sehemu ya **Historia ya Kutazama** inaonyesha video 5 za mwisho ulizotazama, pamoja na picha ndogo na muda. Bonyeza video yoyote ili kuendelea kucheza kutoka ulipoacha, huku historia yako yote ya kutazama ikipakiwa kama foleni.

Bonyeza **Ona Zote** kufungua ukurasa kamili wa [Historia ya Kutazama](/docs/media/watch-history), ambapo unaweza kuvinjari, kutafuta, na kucheza tena kila video uliyotazama katika lugha hii.

## Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa

Sehemu ya **Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa** inaonyesha maneno yako 10 ya hivi karibuni uliyohifadhi kwa lugha lengwa ya sasa. Kila ingizo linaonyesha neno na video ilikotoka — bonyeza neno kufungua ingizo lake la kamusi, au bonyeza **Ona Zote** kuvinjari msamiati wako wote kwenye ukurasa wa [Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa](/docs/vocab/saved-words).

## Vidokezo

- Kiwango chako cha lugha ni kwa kila lugha — kuweka kiwango chako cha Kichina hadi HSK 3 hakuathiri kiwango chako cha Kijapani
- Sehemu za Historia ya Kutazama na Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa zinaonyesha maudhui ya lugha lengwa ya sasa pekee — badilisha L2 kwenye kichwa ili kuona lugha nyingine
- Dhamana ya kurudishiwa pesa ndani ya siku 14. Una maswali? [Wasiliana nasi](mailto:jon.long@zerotohero.ca)
- Kughairi kufanywa upya kiotomatiki kunaweka vipengele vyako vya Pro vikiwa amilifu hadi mwisho wa kipindi chako cha malipo — hakuna kinachokatizwa mapema

title: Usajili
content:
# Usajili

Choose a plan, manage your subscription, and understand what you get with Pro.

## Free vs Pro

Language Player is free to use, with an optional Pro upgrade that unlocks the full experience.

| Feature | Free | Pro |
|---|---|---|
| 600,000+ videos in 207+ languages | ✅ | ✅ |
| Dictionary with collocations, examples, and verb inflections | ✅ | ✅ |
| Chinese character decomposition | ✅ | ✅ |
| Interactive transcripts with translation | First 10 lines | Full transcript |
| Word examples in videos | 2 per word | Hundreds per word |
| AI-powered word explanations | — | ✅ |
| Priority support | — | ✅ |

## Plans

| Plan | Price | Billing |
|---|---|---|
| **Monthly** | US$10/mo | Inatozwa kila mwezi. Cancel anytime. |
| **Annual** | US$90/yr | Inatozwa kila mwaka. Save 25% vs monthly. |
| **Lifetime** | US$169 | Haiwezi Kuisha. Lipa mara moja, pata ufikiaji wa milele. |

All plans include a Dhamana ya kurudishiwa pesa ndani ya siku 14. Una maswali?

## How to Upgrade

1. Open your **[Wasifu](/docs/account/profile)** from the top-right menu
2. Scroll to the **Subscription** section
3. Click **Pata Toleo la Pro** (or **Boresha** if you're already on a Pro plan)
4. Choose a plan — Monthly, Annual, or Lifetime
5. Pick a payment method and complete checkout

After payment, you'll be redirected to a confirmation page. Your Pro features are active immediately — no need to log out or refresh.

## Payment Methods

| Method | Available for | Currency |
|---|---|---|
| Kadi ya Mkopo | All plans | USD |
| WeChat Pay | All plans | CNY |
| Alipay | All plans | CNY |
| PayPal pia inapatikana kwa ununuzi wa maisha yote. | Lifetime only | USD |

Credit card payments are processed securely through Stripe. Your card details are never stored on our servers.

## Managing Your Subscription

### Check Your Status

Your current plan, billing cycle, and renewal date are shown on your **[Wasifu](/docs/account/profile)** page under the Subscription section.

### Cancel Auto-Renewal

Monthly and Annual plans renew automatically. To stop auto-renewal:

1. Go to your **[Wasifu](/docs/account/profile)**
2. In the Subscription section, click **Ghairi Urejeshaji Kiotomatiki**
3. Confirm by clicking **Thibitisha Kufutwa**

Your Pro access continues until the end of your current billing period. After that, your account returns to Free and you'll keep all your saved words, watch history, and settings.

### Renew an Expired Subscription

If your Pro subscription expired, click **Sasisha** on your profile page to subscribe again. All your data is preserved — nothing is lost when a subscription lapses.

### Upgrade Your Plan

You can upgrade from Monthly to Annual or Lifetime at any time:

1. Go to your **[Wasifu](/docs/account/profile)**
2. Click **Boresha** in the Subscription section
3. Choose your new plan and complete payment

When you upgrade, the remaining value of your current plan is credited toward the new one. Your new billing cycle starts immediately.

## Tips

- The Boresha hadi Pro page shows real-time prices — no hidden fees or taxes added at checkout
- Lifetime is a one-time payment with no recurring charges — 1. Ukidhani utaishi zaidi ya miaka 2.4.
- Cancelling auto-renewal doesn't delete anything — you keep full Pro access until the period ends
- If you run into payment issues, contact [Usaidizi wa barua pepe](mailto:jon.long@zerotohero.ca) and we'll help within 24 hours

title: Language Switcher
content:
# Language Switcher

Kicheza Lugha inafanya kazi kwa jozi ya lugha — lugha unayozungumza na lugha unayojifunza. Kila kitu katika programu hii kimejengwa kuzunguka jozi hii: kiolesura kinaonekana kwa lugha yako, video na maingizo ya kamusi yako katika lugha unayojifunza, na tafsiri zinakwenda kati ya hizo mbili.

Unachagua jozi yako unapojisajili kwa mara ya kwanza, na unaweza kuibadilisha wakati wowote kutoka kwenye kichwa.

## Jozi Yako ya Lugha

Kicheza Lugha hutumia lugha mbili nyakati zote:

- **L1** — Lugha yako ya asili. Hii ndiyo kiolesura, menyu, na vitufe vinavyotumia. Pia ndiyo lugha ambayo tafsiri zinaingia.
- **L2** — Lugha unayojifunza. Video, manukuu, na matokeo ya kamusi yote yako katika lugha hii.

Kwa mfano, ukizungumza Kiingereza na unajifunza Kijapani, jozi yako ni Kiingereza → Kijapani. Kiolesura kiko katika Kiingereza, video zinacheza kwa Kijapani, na tafsiri zinakwenda kutoka Kijapani hadi Kiingereza.

## Ziara ya Kwanza — Kujisajili

Mara ya kwanza ukifungua Kicheza Lugha, utaona kiteua lugha. Ina safu mbili pembeni:

- **Safu ya kushoto** — **Ninazungumza**. Chagua lugha unayotumia kwa urahisi zaidi. Hii inakuwa L1 yako.
- **Safu ya kulia** — **Ninajifunza**. Chagua lugha unayotaka kujifunza. Hii inakuwa L2 yako.

Kila safu ina upau wa utafutaji juu. Anza kuandika kutafuta lugha yako, au tembeza kupitia orodha. Lugha maarufu zinaonyeshwa kwanza, zikifuatiwa na lugha zote zinazopatikana kwa mpangilio wa alfabeti.

Bofya lugha kuichagua. Chaguo lako linaangaziwa — bluu kwa L1, machungwa kwa L2. Ukishachagua zote mbili, kitufe cha **Endelea** kinaonekana chini. Bofya ili kuanza kutumia Kicheza Lugha na jozi yako uliyochagua.

### Chaguo la Mashine ya Uandishi wa Kichina

Ukichagua Kichina kama L2 yako, kibadilishaji mashine ya uandishi kinaonekana kati ya safu hizo mbili baada ya kuichagua. Chagua kati ya:

- **简体 Kirahisi** — Herufi zinazotumiwa China Bara na Singapore
- **繁體 Kiasili** — Herufi zinazotumiwa Taiwan, Hong Kong, na Macau

Unaweza kubadilisha upendeleo huu baadaye katika [Mipangilio](/docs/general/settings), chini ya chaguo la **Seti ya herufi** katika kichupo cha Onyesha.

## Kubadilisha Lugha — Kibadilishaji katika Kichwa

Ukishapita hatua ya kujisajili, jozi yako ya sasa ya lugha inaonyeshwa kwenye kichwa juu ya kila ukurasa. Inaonekana kama lebo mbili za lugha na kitufe cha kubadilishana kati yao.

Bofya lebo yoyote ya lugha kufungua orodha kunjuzi. Orodha hiyo ina upau wa utafutaji na orodha ya kutembeza — sehemu za Lugha Maarufu na Lugha Zote sawa na ukurasa wa kujisajili. Bofya lugha mpya kubadilisha. Ukurasa unapakia upya na jozi yako mpya, na kila kitu kinasasishwa: lugha ya kiolesura, video zinazoonyeshwa, kamusi, na maneno yako yaliyohifadhiwa.

### Kubadilishana Lugha

Kati ya lebo hizo mbili za lugha kuna kitufe cha kubadilishana (mishale miwili, ↔). Bofya ili kubadilisha jozi yako — L1 yako inakuwa L2 yako, na L2 yako inakuwa L1 yako. Hii inafanya kazi tu ikiwa L2 yako pia inapatikana kama lugha ya kiolesura (mojawapo ya lugha 31 za UI zinazotumika). Ikiwa sivyo, kitufe hakina athari.

Kubadilishana ni muhimu unapotaka kuona programu kutoka mtazamo mwingine — kwa mfano, ukiwa mzungumzaji wa Kijapani unajifunza Kiingereza, badilishana ili kuona kiolesura kwa Kijapani na maingizo ya kamusi kwa Kiingereza.

## Nini Hubadilika Ukibadilisha

Kubadilisha jozi yako ya lugha huathiri karibu kila kitu katika programu:

| Kinachobadilika | Maelezo |
|---|---|
| **Lugha ya kiolesura** | Menyu zote, vitufe, na lebo zinabadilika hadi L1 yako mpya |
| **Video** | Chunguza, Vipindi vya TV, na Runinga ya Moja kwa Moja zinaonyesha maudhui katika L2 yako mpya |
| **Kamusi** | Kamusi inabadilika hadi jozi yako mpya ya L2 ↔ L1. Ufafanuzi unaonekana katika L1 yako mpya |
| **Maneno yaliyohifadhiwa** | Orodha za msamiati ni kwa kila lugha. Kubadilisha hadi L2 tofauti kunaonyesha maneno yaliyohifadhiwa ya lugha hiyo |
| **Mipangilio** | Mipangilio ya kila lugha (kama fonetiki na sauti) inabadilika ili kuendana na L2 yako mpya |
| **Utafutaji** | Utafutaji wa video na kamusi unalenga L2 yako mpya |

Historia yako ya kutazama, maneno yaliyohifadhiwa, na mipangilio yote imehifadhiwa kwa kila lugha — hakuna kinachopotea unapobadilisha.

## Kutafuta Lugha

Kichagua lugha cha kujisajili na orodha kunjuzi ya kichwa zote zina upau wa utafutaji. Andika ili kuchuja orodha ya lugha papo hapo. Utafutaji unalingana na:

- Jina la lugha kwa mashine yake ya uandishi (mfano, 日本語, Español)
- Jina lililobadilishwa kwa lugha yako ya sasa ya kiolesura
- Jina la Kiingereza
- Msimbo wa lugha wa ISO (mfano, `ja`, `es`, `zh`)

Futa utafutaji ili kuona orodha kamili tena huku Lugha Maarufu zikiwa juu.

## Vidokezo

- Chagua **lugha yako yenye nguvu zaidi** kama L1, si ile unayotakiwa kuzungumza. Tafsiri sahihi zinategemea hili.
- Tumia kitufe cha kubadilishana kuona jinsi programu inavyoonekana katika L2 yako — ni njia ya haraka ya kupima uelewa wako wa kusoma.
- Ukijifunza lugha nyingi, badilisha kati yao kutoka kwenye kichwa. Maendeleo yako kwa kila moja yanahifadhiwa tofauti.
- Kibadilishaji mashine ya uandishi cha Kichina kinakumbuka chaguo lako, kwa hivyo unahitaji kukiseta mara moja tu.
- Jozi yako ya lugha iliyotumiwa mara ya mwisho imehifadhiwa kwenye kivinjari chako. Wakati ujao utakapotembelea, utaelekezwa moja kwa moja kwenye maudhui yako — hakuna haja ya kuchagua tena.

title: Getting Started
content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to Kicheza Lugha — learn any language by watching real videos with interactive subtitles. Click any word to see its definition, save it to your vocabulary, and let the app schedule reviews so you never forget.

This guide walks you through everything from creating an account to watching your first video.

## Create a Free Account

You can browse Kicheza Lugha without an account, but signing up lets you save words, track your watch history, and sync your progress across devices.

1. Click **Jiandikishe** in the top-right corner
2. Enter your email and a password
3. Check your inbox for a verification code — enter it to confirm your email
4. You're in! Your account is free and stays free

A Akaunti Bure gives you access to 600,000+ videos across 207+ languages, the full dictionary, and spaced repetition review. See [Usajili](/docs/account/billing) for what's included and how to upgrade if you want more.

## Choose Your Languages

Kicheza Lugha works with a **language pair** — your native language (L1) and the language you're learning (L2). Pick both to get started.

### First Time

When you sign up, you'll see the language selection page with two columns:

- **Left column** — Chagua lugha zako ili kuanza (your native language). This sets the app's interface language.
- **Right column** — your target language (the one you want to learn).

Type to search. You can search by the language's **native name**, its **English name**, or its **ISO code**. For example, to find Kijapani, you can type `日本語`, `Japanese`, or `ja`.

If you're learning Kichina, a toggle appears below the search bar to switch between Simplified and Traditional characters.

Click a language in each column, then click **Anza Kujifunza** at the bottom. Your choices are saved in the URL and remembered across sessions.

### Switching Later

You can change either language at any time from the header. Click the language name in the top bar to open a dropdown, or use the **swap button** between the two language labels to flip your pair (L1 ↔ L2). See [[?title.language_switcher]](/docs/general/language-switcher) for all the ways to switch.

## Watch Your First Video

The fastest way to get started:

1. Go to **Chunguza** from the sidebar or navigation menu
2. You'll see a grid of video cards — each one has a thumbnail, a difficulty badge (like CEFR A1 or HSK 3), and a view count
3. Use the **level filter pills** at the top to find videos at your proficiency level
4. Click any video to open the [Kusoma](/docs/media/video-player)

The video player shows interactive subtitles beside the video. Every word is clickable — tap a word to see its definition, pronunciation, and a bookmark button to save it to your vocabulary list. Translations appear below each subtitle line in your native language.

See [Chunguza](/docs/media/explore) for details on filtering, channel subscriptions, and difficulty scoring.

## What You Can Do

Kicheza Lugha has more than just videos. Here's what's available:

| Feature | What it does | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **Interactive video player** | Watch with dual subtitles and clickable words | [Chunguza](/docs/media/explore) → click any video |
| **Kamusi** | Look up any word, see conjugations, get AI explanations | **Msamiati** → [Kamusi](/docs/vocab/dictionary) |
| **Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa** | Build your personal vocabulary list | Click the bookmark icon on any word |
| **Mapitio** | Spaced repetition flashcards for your saved words | **Msamiati** → [Mapitio](/docs/vocab/review) |
| **Kisoma** | Paste or write text, read with interactive word lookup | **Kusoma** → [Kisoma](/docs/reading/reader) |
| **Vipindi vya TV** | Browse TV series with full episode queues | **Vidokezo** → [Vipindi vya TV](/docs/media/tv-shows) |
| **Runinga ya Moja kwa Moja** | Stream live broadcasts in your target language | [Runinga ya Moja kwa Moja](/docs/media/live-tv) |
| **Muziki na Burudani** | Music videos and entertainment content | [Muziki na Burudani](/docs/media/music) |
| **Tafuta** | Find videos by title or paste any YouTube URL | [Tafuta](/docs/media/search) |

## Build Your Vocabulary

As you watch, click unfamiliar words and click the bookmark icon to save them. Over time, you'll build a personal vocabulary list organized by the date you saved each word.

Your saved words feed into the [Mapitio](/docs/vocab/review) system — the app schedules flashcards so you review each word just before you'd forget it. Each card shows the original sentence where you found the word, so you're always learning in context.

See [Popup Dictionary](/docs/vocab/popup-dictionary) for how word lookup works, and [Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa](/docs/vocab/saved-words) for managing your vocabulary list.

## Free vs Pro

Kicheza Lugha is free to use. The Pro upgrade unlocks:

- Full interactive transcripts with translation (free accounts see the first 10 lines)
- Hundreds of video examples per word (free accounts see 2)
- AI-powered word explanations
- Priority support

Plans start at US$10/mo with a Dhamana ya kurudishiwa pesa ndani ya siku 14. Una maswali?. See [Usajili](/docs/account/billing) for all plans, payment methods, and what's included.

## Customize Your Experience

Open [Mipangilio](/docs/general/settings) from your profile menu to adjust:

- **Onyesha** — Theme, translation toggle, phonetics (pinyin, furigana), font size, character set
- **Uchezaji** — Playback speed, auto-pause, karaoke animation, smooth scrolling
- **Usemi** — Text-to-speech voice and rate for word pronunciation
- **Hakiki** — How many new vocabulary cards to introduce per day (default: 20)

Mipangilio save automatically and sync across your devices when you're signed in.

## Tips

- **Click everything.** Words in subtitles, words in the dictionary, words in your saved list — they're all interactive.
- **Set your proficiency level** on your [Wasifu](/docs/account/profile) so Chunguza starts with the right level filter.
- **Save words as you watch**, not after. The bookmark button is one click and keeps you in the flow.
- **Use keyboard shortcuts** in the video player — Space for play/pause, ←/→ to jump between subtitle lines, R to rewind 2 seconds.
- **Switch languages anytime** from the header. Your settings, saved words, and watch history are tracked per language pair.
- **Read the docs** — each feature page ends with a Tips section that covers shortcuts and workflows you might miss otherwise.

title: Chunguza
content:
# Chunguza

Gundua video katika lugha unayolenga, zinazolingana na kiwango chako.

## Jinsi Inavyofanya Kazi

Ukurasa wa Chunguza unaonyesha video zinazopendekezwa kwa jozi lako la lugha. Kila video inatoka YouTube na imechaguliwa kwa ajili ya kujifunza lugha — hatujumuishi muziki na maudhui ya burudani (yale yana ukurasa wao wa [Muziki na Burudani](/docs/media/music)).

Video zimepangwa kwa umaarufu na kuchujwa kwa ugumu unapochagua kiwango. Mfumo wa mapendekezo hujifunza kutoka kwenye historia yako ya utazamaji, hivyo video ulizoshazitazama hazitaonekana tena.

## Kichujio cha Kiwango

Vidonge vilivyo juu hukuruhusu kuchuja kwa kiwango cha ustadi. Lebo hujirekebisha kulingana na lugha unayolenga:

| L2 | Vidonge vinaonyesha |
|---|---|
| Kichina | HSK 1 → HSK 7-9 |
| Kijapani | JLPT Pre-N5 → N1 |
| Kikorea | TOPIK Pre-1 → 6 |
| Kiingereza | IELTS 1 → 9 |
| Nyingine | CEFR Pre-A1 → C2 |

Bonyeza kidonge ili kuchuja video kwa kiwango hicho. Bonyeza **Zote** ili kuona video za kila kiwango. Kichujio kinakumbuka chaguo lako unapovinjari, na kinarejea kwenye kiwango chako cha ustadi kilichohifadhiwa unaporudi.

## Kadi za Video

Kila kadi inaonyesha **beji ya kiwango** yenye rangi kwenye kona ya juu-kushoto. Tazama [Jinsi Ugumu Unavyohesabiwa](#jinsi-ugumu-unavyohesabiwa) kwa jinsi viwango hivi vinavyoamuliwa.

Bonyeza kadi yoyote ili kufungua kicheza video. Unaporudi kwenye Chunguza, mahali pako kwenye orodha pahahifadhiwa haswa pale ulipoachia.

### Vitendo vya Kituo

Kila kadi ya video ina menyu ya **…** kwenye kona ya juu-kulia yenye chaguo mbili:

- **Jisajili** — Video kutoka kituo hiki zitaonekana kwanza kwenye mapendekezo yako. Takriban 25% ya kila ukurasa umehifadhiwa kwa vituo ulivyojisajili, kwa hivyo utaona kila wakati maudhui yao mapya karibu na juu. Hii ni muhimu kwa vituo unavyotaka kufuatilia mara kwa mara — vyombo vya habari, waundaji wa elimu, au wanablogu wako wapendwa.

- **Sina nia** — Video kutoka kituo hiki zitafichwa kabisa kwenye mapendekezo yako. Hii ni muhimu kwa vituo vinavyoonekana mara kwa mara lakini havifai — labda maudhui yana kiwango cha juu sana, rahisi sana, au hayahusiani na malengo yako ya kujifunza.

Mapendeleo yako ya vituo yanahifadhiwa kwa kila lugha, kwa hivyo kujisajili kwa kituo cha habari cha Kijapani hakuathiri mapendekezo yako ya Kikorea. Unaweza kusimamia usajili wako wote na vituo vilivyofichwa kutoka kwa wasifu wako.

## Jinsi Ugumu Unavyohesabiwa

Kila video inapewa alama ya ugumu kulingana na mzunguko wa maneno katika manukuu yake. Mfumo hutumia orodha za mzunguko wa Zipf kwa kila lugha — majedwali makubwa yanayoorodhesha jinsi kila neno linavyojulikana katika maandishi halisi. Neno kama "the" lina mzunguko wa juu (linajulikana), wakati "sesquipedalian" lina mzunguko wa chini (adimu).

Alama zimegawanywa katika viwango 7 kwa kutumia vizingiti vya kielelezo: 1/127 ya maneno yenye mzunguko wa juu zaidi ni kiwango cha 1, 2/127 inayofuata ni kiwango cha 2, 4/123 inayofuata ni kiwango cha 3, na kadhalika — ikiongezeka mara mbili kila hatua. Kiwango cha 7 kinashughulikia 64/127 ya maneno adimu zaidi.

Kwa sababu usambazaji wa mzunguko wa maneno hutofautiana kati ya lugha, vizingiti ni **maalum kwa lugha**. Alama ya ugumu ya 0.008 katika Kijapani inaenda kwa JLPT N1 (kiwango cha 6), wakati alama hiyo hiyo katika Kikorea inaenda kwa TOPIK 3 (kiwango cha 4). Sehemu ya mbele inachukua vizingiti hivi mara moja kutoka sehemu ya nyuma na kuvitumia kuweka lebo kwenye kila kadi ya video.

Mapendeleo yako ya vituo yanahifadhiwa kwa kila lugha, kwa hivyo kujisajili kwa kituo cha habari cha Kijapani hakuathiri mapendekezo yako ya Kikorea. Unaweza kusimamia usajili wako wote na vituo vilivyofichwa kutoka kwa wasifu wako.

## Vidokezo

- Weka kiwango chako cha ustadi kwenye [ukurasa wako wa wasifu](/docs/account/profile) ili Chunguza ianze na kichujio sahihi
- Badilisha viwango ili kupata maudhui rahisi au magumu zaidi — kurudi nyuma ni mara moja
- Tumia [Muziki na Burudani](/docs/media/music) kwa video za muziki na maudhui ya burudani
- Historia yako ya utazamaji ni ya faragha na inatumika tu kuzuia kuonyesha video moja mara mbili

title: Subtitle Search
content:
# Subtitle Search

Search for any word or phrase across millions of video subtitles. See how native speakers actually use it — in context, with timestamps, ready to watch.

## How It Works

Type a word or phrase in your target language, and Language Player searches every subtitle line in its video database. Results show you exactly where that word appears, in which video, and at what moment.

You can access subtitle search in two ways:

- **From a dictionary entry** — open the **Examples from Videos** tab on any dictionary entry page. It searches for the headword across all subtitles automatically.
- **From the explore page** — use the search bar on the Explore page to find words or phrases in video subtitles.

## Understanding the Results

Each result shows:

- **The subtitle line** containing your search term, with the matching word or phrase highlighted.
- **The video title** — click it to open that video.
- **A timestamp link** — click it to jump directly to the moment the line is spoken. The video starts playing from that exact subtitle line.

Results are grouped by video. If a word appears multiple times in the same video, each occurrence gets its own result line. This way you can compare different contexts for the same word in the same video.

## Exact vs. Partial Matching

By default, the search matches inflected and conjugated forms of your word. For example, searching for "食べる" (to eat) also finds "食べた" (ate), "食べます" (eat, polite), and other forms.

Toggle **Exact Match** to restrict results to the exact form you typed. This is useful when you want to see how a specific conjugation or tense is used, rather than all forms of the word.

## Tips

- **Use it to verify usage** — before using a new word yourself, check how native speakers use it in real conversations.
- **Jump between examples** — click different timestamps for the same word to hear it in different contexts, accents, and speaking speeds.
- **Combine with saved words** — save a word from the dictionary, then use the Examples tab to find videos where you can hear it in action.

title: Vipindi vya TV
content:
# Vipindi vya TV

Vinjari vipindi vya televisheni katika lugha unayolenga na tazama vipindi kamili kwa manukuu shirikishi ya pande mbili.

## Jinsi Inavyofanya Kazi

Ukurasa wa Vipindi vya TV unaorodhesha kila mfululizo wa TV unaopatikana katika lugha unayolenga. Kila kipindi ni kadi yenye picha ya jalada, jina la kipindi, mwaka wa kutolewa, na nchi asili. Bofya kipindi chochote kuona vipindi vyake.

Vipindi vimepangwa kwa mfululizo na kuhesabiwa kwa mpangilio. Bofya kipindi chochote kukifungua kwenye [Kicheza Video](/docs/media/video-player) — kila kipindi kinapata manukuu shirikishi na utafiti wa kamusi, kama video nyingine yoyote.

Unapocheza kipindi, mfululizo mzima hupangwa kiotomatiki. Tumia vitufe vya **Iliyopita** na **Inayofuata** kuhama kati ya vipindi bila kurudi kwenye orodha.

## Kutafuta Vipindi

### Tafuta

Tumia upau wa utafutaji juu ya ukurasa kutafuta kipindi kwa jina. Andika neno kuu katika lugha unayolenga na orodha huchuja papo hapo — hakuna haja ya kubonyeza Enter.

### Panga

Panga orodha kwa njia tatu:

- **Yaliyoangaliwa zaidi** — vipindi vilivyotazamwa zaidi kwanza. Vizuri kwa kutafuta mfululizo maarufu.
- **Kichwa** — kialfabeti kwa jina la kipindi. Vizuri unapojua jina.
- **Mwaka** — vipindi vipya zaidi kwanza. Vizuri kwa kugundua mfululizo wa hivi karibuni.

### Kichujio cha Eneo

Baadhi ya lugha huzungumzwa katika nchi nyingi. Kwa mfano, TV ya Kifaransa hutoka Ufaransa, Ubelgiji, Uswisi, na Kanada. Kichujio cha **CHUJA KWA ENEO** hukuruhusu kupunguza orodha kwa vipindi kutoka nchi maalum. Kinaonekana tu wakati zaidi ya eneo moja linapatikana.

## Mwonekano wa Kipindi

Baada ya kubofya kipindi, utaona:

- **Jina la kipindi** na nchi asili juu
- Orodha ya **vipindi vyote**, vilivyohesabiwa na kupangwa kwa jina
- Kila safu ya kipindi inaonyesha picha ndogo, jina, muda, na idadi ya watazamaji

Bofya kipindi chochote kuanza kutazama. Kicheza video kinafunguliwa na mfululizo mzima umepangwa — unaweza kuruka kati ya vipindi kwa vidhibiti vya Iliyopita/Inayofuata kwenye kicheza.

Kitufe cha **Nyuma** juu kinakurudisha kwenye orodha ya vipindi. Mahali pako kwenye orodha huhifadhiwa, ili uweze kuchagua kipindi kingine haraka bila kuanza upya.

## Vidokezo

- Panga kwa **Yaliyoangaliwa zaidi** unapochunguza lugha mpya — vipindi maarufu huwa na ubora bora wa manukuu
- Weka alamisho kwenye ukurasa wa kipindi ili kuruka moja kwa moja kwenye vipindi vyake bila kutafuta
- Mfululizo mzima hujipanga unapocheza kipindi, hivyo unaweza kutazama mfululizo bila usumbufu
- Tumia ukurasa wa [Chunguza](/docs/media/explore) kwa video binafsi, si mfululizo kamili wa TV
- Ikiwa kipindi kina vipindi kwa lugha nyingi, chuja kwa eneo ili kuona tu vipindi katika lugha unayolenga

title: Historia ya Kutazama
content:
# Historia ya Kutazama

Kila video unayotazama huhifadhiwa kwenye historia yako. Anza kutoka ulipoishia, rudia vipendwa, na fuatilia ulichokuwa unakiona.

## Jinsi Inavyofanya Kazi

Kicheza Lugha huhifadhi historia yako ya utazamaji kiotomatiki. Kila baada ya sekunde 15 ukiwa unatazama video, nafasi yako ya sasa ya uchezaji inarekodiwa. Utakaporudi kwenye video baadaye, unaweza kuendelea kutoka ulipoishia.

Historia yako ni ya lugha-kwa-lugha — kubadili kutoka Kijapani hadi Kikorea kunaonyesha orodha tofauti. Pia inahusishwa na akaunti yako, hivyo inakufuata kwenye vifaa vyote.

## Kuangalia Historia Yako

Fungua **Historia ya Kutazama** kutoka kwenye menyu kuu ya urambazaji. Utaona orodha ya kila video uliyotazama katika lugha yako ya sasa ya lengo, zikiwa za hivi karibuni mwanzoni.

Kila kipengee kinaonyesha:

- **Picha ndogo** yenye kitufe cha kucheza kinachoonekana unapoelea juu yake
- **Kichwa cha video**
- **Muda** na **tarehe** uliyoitazama
- **Upau wa maendeleo** kwenye picha ndogo — mstari wa rangi chini unaoonyesha umetazama kiasi gani
- **Asilimia** kama "{pct} imetazamwa" ili ujue mahali pako kwa mtazamo mmoja

Bofya video yoyote ili kuendelea kutazama. Historia nzima inakuwa msururu wako, hivyo unaweza kuruka kati ya video ulizotazama hivi karibuni kwa vidhibiti vya Iliyotangulia/Ijayo kwenye kicheza.

## Mahali Historia Inapoonekana

Historia yako ya utazamaji inaonekana katika sehemu mbili:

- **Ukurasa wa Historia ya Kutazama** — historia yako kamili ya lugha ya sasa, ikiwa na kila video uliyotazama
- **[Wasifu](/docs/account/profile) yako** — video 5 za mwisho ulizotazama, zikiwa na kiungo cha kuona orodha kamili

## Kuendeleza Uchezaji

Unapofungua tena video uliyotazama hapo awali, kicheza kinarejesha nafasi yako iliyohifadhiwa. Upau wa maendeleo kwenye upau wa kutafuta na asilimia kwenye orodha yako ya historia vyote vinaonyesha ulipoishia.

Nafasi huhifadhiwa hata ukifunga kichupo katikati ya video. Mradi tu ulikuwa unatazama kwa angalau sekunde chache, nafasi yako inabakia.

## Faragha

Historia yako ya utazamaji ni ya faragha. Inaonekana kwako tu ukiwa umeingia. Inatumika kwa:

- Kuonyesha video zako za hivi karibuni kwa ufikiaji rahisi
- Kuepuka kupendekeza video ulizoshaziona kwenye ukurasa wa [Chunguza](/docs/media/explore)

## Vidokezo

- Tumia historia yako kama njia ya haraka ya kurudia video usiyoimaliza
- Upau wa maendeleo kwenye kila picha ndogo unakupa hisia ya papo hapo ya ulipoishia
- Badilisha lugha ili kuona historia tofauti kwa kila lugha unayojifunza
- Historia yako ina mipaka ya L2 yako ya sasa — hutaona video za Kijapani ukiwa katika hali ya Kikorea

title: Kisoma EPUB
content:
# Kisoma EPUB

Read EPUB e-books with interactive word lookup, per-block translation, and progress that persists across sessions.

## Getting Started

### Upload an EPUB

1. Navigate to **Reading → Kisoma EPUB** in the menu
2. **Drag & drop** an `.epub` file onto the upload zone, or click **Browse** to select one from your device
3. The cover image appears — tap it to start reading

The file is stored locally in your browser (IndexedDB). Next time you visit, it will load automatically and you'll resume from where you left off.

### Navigation

| Feature | How |
|---|---|
| **Next/Previous chapter** | Use the buttons at the top of the sidebar, or click chapter titles in the table of contents |
| **Collapse/expand sidebar** | Click the `≡` toggle in the top-right corner of the title bar |
| **Close the book** | Click `✕ Close` in the title bar to return to the upload screen |
| **Page turn within a chapter** | Use `←` / `→` keys or the page controls at the bottom of the reader |
| **Table of contents** | Nested TOC items are indented for easy navigation |

### Reading Features

- **Click any word** to look up its dictionary definition, pronunciation, and example sentences
- **Per-block translation** — Click the `Languages` icon in the page nav bar to translate the current page's visible blocks
- **Auto-translate on page turn** — When translation is enabled, the next page's content is translated automatically
- **Inline translation display** — On wide screens, original and translation appear side by side. On narrow screens, translation appears below each block.

## Supported Formats

- **`.epub`** — Standard EPUB files (EPUB 2 and EPUB 3)
- **`.epub` with furigana/ruby** — Kijapani books with ruby annotations are stripped of furigana, keeping only the base kanji text
- **Vertical text** — Books with CSS `writing-mode: vertical-rl` are detected (display uses standard horizontal layout for readability)

## Progress Saving

Your reading position is saved automatically in your browser:

- **Current chapter** is saved when you switch chapters
- **Text anchor** (the first ~40 characters of the visible page) is saved when you turn pages
- On your next visit, the book reloads and scrolls to the correct position

**Note:** Progress is stored locally (IndexedDB), not synced to the cloud. Only one book is stored at a time — uploading a new one replaces the previous.

## Getting Free EPUBs

- **[Project Gutenberg](https://www.gutenberg.org/)** — 70,000+ free ebooks in many languages (look for " EPUB with images" or " Plain Text UTF-8")
- **[Aozora Bunko](https://www.aozora.gr.jp/)** (青空文庫) — Kijapani literature, public domain
- **[Wikisource](https://wikisource.org/)** — Public domain texts in many languages, downloadable as EPUB

title: Notes Reader
content:
# Notes Reader

Paste or write text in your target language and read it with interactive word lookup, per-block translations, and a built-in note-taking system.

## How It Works

The Notes Reader lets you bring your own text — paste an article, type a journal entry, or load a web page. The reader tokenizes the text so every word becomes clickable. You can look up words in the dictionary, see translations, and save everything as a note that you can return to later.

## Getting Text In

There are three ways to add text:

- **Write or paste** — Switch to the Edit tab and type or paste directly into the text area. Markdown formatting is supported (headings, lists, blockquotes, code, tables).
- **Load a URL** — Paste a web page URL into the URL input at the top. The reader fetches the page and converts it to readable text.
- **Jaza kwa sampuli** — Click "Fill with sample" to load a short example text in your target language, useful for exploring the reader's features.

Each piece of text you work with is saved as a **note**. All your notes appear in the sidebar on the left. Click any note to load it back — your text, translations, and reading position are remembered.

## Reading

### Edit Mode

The **Edit** tab is where you add or edit text. Type directly into the text area, or paste from an external source. When you're ready to read, click **Tenganisha** to make every word interactive.

Markdown is fully supported. Use `#` for headings, `*` or `-` for lists, `>` for blockquotes, and backticks for code. The reader preserves your formatting in the Read tab.

### Read Mode

The **Read** tab shows your text paginated to fit the screen. Each page shows as much text as fits without scrolling.

**Navigate pages** with the `←` `→` buttons at the bottom, or use your keyboard: `←` and `↑` for previous page, `→`, `↓`, and `Space` for next page.

## Interactive Words

After tokenization, every word is clickable. Tap or click any word to open the [Popup Dictionary](/docs/vocab/popup-dictionary). You can see definitions, pronunciations, example sentences, and save the word to your vocabulary.

Words you've previously saved are shown with a quick gloss — the first definition appears inline next to the word.

## Tafsiri

Toggle the **Tafsiri** switch in the page navigation bar to show or hide translations. This setting is remembered across sessions.

When enabled, translations appear inline — on wide screens they sit next to the original text, and on narrow screens they appear below each paragraph. Translations are fetched per page and appear as you navigate.

**Per-block actions** let you interact with each paragraph individually. Hover over any text block to reveal a `⋮` menu with these options:

- **Nakala** — Copy the text to your clipboard
- **Sema** — Read the text aloud with text-to-speech
- **Acha DeepSeek Aelezee** — Get an AI breakdown of the text's grammar and structure
- **Tafsiri** — See a popup translation for just that block

## Managing Notes

All your notes appear in the sidebar. You can:

- **Create a new note** — Click "New Note" to start with a blank text area
- **Rename a note** — Click the `⋮` next to a note and choose Rename
- **Delete a note** — Click the `⋮` next to a note and choose Delete

Your note is saved as you type. There's no save button — changes are saved to your account after a brief pause. When you switch between notes or close the reader, your latest changes are always preserved.

## See Also

- [Kisoma wavuti](/docs/reading/web-reader) — Read web articles by URL without saving notes
- [Kisoma EPUB](/docs/reading/epub) — Read EPUB books with chapter navigation
- [Popup Dictionary](/docs/vocab/popup-dictionary) — How clicking words works
- [Kamusi](/docs/vocab/dictionary) — Full dictionary features

## Tips

- Use markdown headings to organize longer texts — they help break content into readable sections
- The `⋮` menu on each block is a quick way to look up a single word or phrase without switching tabs
- Keyboard navigation (`←` `→`) is faster than clicking page buttons for extended reading
- Rename your notes to something descriptive — "Chapter 3 notes" is easier to find later than "Untitled"

title: Web Reader
content:
# Web Reader

Paste a URL and read any web page in your target language with interactive word lookup and translations. No account needed.

## How It Works

Enter the URL of a web page — a news article, a blog post, a Wikipedia page, or any public webpage. The Web Reader fetches the page, strips away navigation and ads, and displays the main content as clean, readable text. Every word becomes clickable for dictionary lookup.

The Web Reader is read-only — it doesn't save anything to your account. If you want to keep your text and come back to it later, use the [Notes Reader](/docs/reading/reader) instead.

## Loading a Page

1. Paste a full URL (starting with `https://`) into the input at the top of the page
2. Click **Pakia**
3. The page content appears as clean text, ready to read

You can also share a link directly. Add `?url=` to the Web Reader URL:

```
/en/zh/web-reader?url=https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/...
```

This loads the page when someone opens the link.

## What Gets Kept

The Web Reader extracts the main content of a page and removes clutter:

- Navigation menus, sidebars, headers, and footers are stripped
- Wikipedia edit links, reference lists, infoboxes, and metadata are removed
- The main article body is preserved — paragraphs, headings, lists, and links
- Relative links are rewritten to point back to the original page

The cleaned content is displayed as formatted text with markdown styling. Headings, bullet lists, blockquotes, and code blocks all render as you'd expect.

## Interactive Words

After the page loads, click **Tenganisha** to make every word interactive. Tap or click any word to open the [Popup Dictionary](/docs/vocab/popup-dictionary). You can see definitions, pronunciations, and example sentences, and save words to your vocabulary.

Words you've previously saved are highlighted — the first definition appears inline next to the word as a quick reminder.

## Translation

Toggle the **Translation** switch to show or hide translations. When enabled, translations appear inline — on wide screens they sit next to the original text, and on narrow screens they appear below each paragraph. Translations load as you navigate between pages.

**Per-block actions** let you interact with each paragraph individually. Hover over any text block to reveal a `⋮` menu with these options:

- **Nakala** — Copy the text to your clipboard
- **Sema** — Read the text aloud with text-to-speech
- **Acha DeepSeek Aelezee** — Get an AI breakdown of the text's grammar and structure
- **Tafsiri** — See a popup translation for just that block

## Page Navigation

The text is paginated to fit your screen. Use the `←` `→` buttons at the bottom to move between pages, or use your keyboard: `←` and `↑` for previous page, `→`, `↓`, and `Space` for next page.

## See Also

- [Notes Reader](/docs/reading/reader) — Write or paste your own text, save notes, and edit content
- [EPUB Reader](/docs/reading/epub) — Read EPUB books with chapter navigation
- [Popup Dictionary](/docs/vocab/popup-dictionary) — How clicking words works

## Tips

- Wikipedia articles work particularly well — the content extraction is tuned for Wikipedia's layout
- Use the `?url=` parameter to bookmark specific articles in your target language
- The Web Reader doesn't save your text — if you want to annotate or come back later, copy the URL and paste it into the Notes Reader instead
- Long articles benefit from keyboard navigation (`←` `→`) for faster reading

title: Kamusi
content:
# Kamusi

The dictionary is your instant word lookup tool. It works everywhere — in subtitles, in search, and on its own dedicated page.

## Looking Up Words

You can look up a word in three ways:

- **Tap any word** in the subtitles or transcript while watching a video. A popup opens with the definition, pronunciation, and a bookmark button. See [Popup Dictionary](/docs/vocab/popup-dictionary) for details.
- **Use the search bar** at the top of the Dictionary page. Type a word in your target language and press Enter.
- **Click a saved word** in your vocabulary list to jump straight to its full entry.

The search bar is always there — it never moves. Whether you're browsing results or reading a full entry, the search bar stays at the top so you can look up another word instantly.

## What You Get

Every entry gives you the essentials up front:

- **Definitions** in your native language, ordered by relevance
- **Pronunciation** — tap the speaker icon to hear it aloud, plus a phonetic guide (Pinyin, Romaji, or IPA)
- **Part of speech** — noun, verb, adjective, and so on
- **Proficiency level** — where the word falls on scales like CEFR (A1–C2), HSK (1–6), or JLPT (N5–N1)

Depending on the language and the word, you might also see:

- **Alternate scripts** — for Kichina, see the word in both simplified and traditional. For Kivietinamu, see the Chữ Hán form. For Kikorea, see the Hanja. You control which scripts appear in [Mipangilio](/settings).
- **Classifiers** — for Kichina, see which measure words go with the noun (like 个, 本, 张). For Kifaransa or Kijerumani, see the grammatical gender.
- **Textbook coverage** — if the word appears in a textbook like the HSK Standard Course, you'll see exactly which book, lesson, and dialog it's from.

### Tabs

The entry page has tabs next to the definitions panel:

- **Mifano kutoka kwa Video** — see real subtitle lines from YouTube videos that contain this word. Great for hearing how it's actually used. Learn more in [Subtitle Search](/docs/vocab/subs-search).
- **Mnyambuliko** — full conjugation or declension tables for verbs and adjectives. Available for Kijapani, Kikorea, Kirusi, Kiukreini, Kiingereza, Kifaransa, Kijerumani, Kihispania, Kiitaliano, and Kiholanzi.
- **AI Explanation** — a plain-Kiingereza breakdown of the word's meaning, usage, and nuances. Generated on the fly by DeepSeek.

## Where Dictionary Entries Come From

Language Player uses curated, versioned dictionaries built from open data:

| Language | Dictionary | Entries |
|---|---|---|
| Kichina | HSK CEDICT | 120,000+ |
| Cantonese | CC-Canto | 50,000+ |
| Kijapani | EDICT | 170,000+ |
| Kikorea | Kengdic | 120,000+ |
| Klingon | Klingonska | 3,000+ |
| 60+ other languages | Wiktionary | varies by language |

If a word isn't in any dictionary — like a rare dialect word or a new slang term — the app asks an AI model to generate a definition on the spot. These AI-generated entries are marked so you know they're not from a curated source.

## Saving Words

Click the bookmark icon on any dictionary entry to save the word to your personal vocabulary list. Your saved words appear in the sidebar while you're browsing the dictionary, so you can quickly jump between them.

Saved words sync across devices if you're logged in. You can browse and manage them on the [Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa](/docs/vocab/saved-words) page. Use the [Mapitio](/docs/vocab/review) feature to practice your saved words with spaced repetition — the app schedules reviews so you see each word just before you'd forget it.

## Tips

- **Click the speaker icon** next to any word to hear its pronunciation. This works in the popup, the search results, and the full entry page.
- **Use the sidebar toggle** (the panel icon in the search bar) to show or hide your saved words list while browsing entries.
- **Search Google Images** from any entry page — the link is at the bottom of the definitions panel. Useful for concrete nouns when a picture is worth a thousand words.
- **If a word's definition seems off**, check the AI Explanation tab. The AI sometimes catches nuances that dictionary definitions miss.
- **Switch scripts** in [Mipangilio](/settings) if you prefer traditional Kichina characters or want to see Hanja alongside Kikorea Hangul.

title: Popup Dictionary
content:
# Popup Dictionary

Click any word in the subtitles or transcript, and a popup opens with everything you need — definitions, pronunciation, and a quick way to save the word.

## How It Works

While watching a video, every word in the subtitles is clickable. When you tap a word:

1. A popup appears next to the word with dictionary entries from our curated dictionaries.
2. If the word isn't in any dictionary, an AI model generates a definition on the spot.
3. You can scroll through multiple entries if the word has several meanings.

The popup stays open until you click outside it or press the close button.

## What's in the Popup

Each dictionary entry in the popup is a compact card showing:

- **Headword** with pronunciation and alternate scripts
- **Definitions** ordered by relevance
- **Part of speech** and proficiency level badges
- **Save button** — click the bookmark icon to add this word to your vocabulary list

Below the dictionary entries, an **AI Explanation** section gives you a plain-language breakdown of the word's meaning and usage. This is especially helpful for words with multiple senses or tricky grammar.

## Saving Words from the Popup

Click the bookmark icon on any entry to save it. The app automatically fetches all inflected and conjugated forms of the word, so every form you encounter in the future will be recognized as saved.

When you save a word from a video, the subtitle line and video title are stored as context. You'll see exactly where you first encountered the word when you browse your [Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa](/docs/vocab/saved-words) list.

## Full Kamusi Entry

Click any entry card in the popup to jump to the full dictionary page. The full page gives you everything the popup does, plus:

- Conjugation tables for verbs and adjectives
- Real video examples from our subtitle database
- Textbook appearances (HSK Standard Course and more)
- A Google Images search link

## Unrecognized Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa

If you have a word saved in your vocabulary but none of the popup's dictionary entries match it, the popup shows an "unrecognized saved word" warning. This can happen when a saved word uses an older ID format that doesn't match the current dictionary. The warning lets you know the word is still in your list — it just couldn't be matched to a specific entry in this popup.

## Tips

- **Click the entry card** (not just the bookmark) to open the full dictionary page for more detail.
- **Save first, read later** — bookmark interesting words as you watch, then review them in your [Maneno Yaliyohifadhiwa](/docs/vocab/saved-words) list after the video.
- **The popup works on any word** — even conjugated verbs, declined nouns, and words with typos. The lemmatizer finds the dictionary form automatically.

title: Mapitio
content:
# Mapitio

Mapitio your saved vocabulary with spaced repetition flashcards.

## How It Works

Each saved word becomes a flashcard. The front shows the sentence where you saved the word — the word itself is highlighted so you can spot it instantly. Your job is to recall the meaning from context.

Tap the card (or press Space) to reveal the back. You'll see the full dictionary entry with definitions, examples, inflections, and an AI explanation. Compare what you recalled with the actual definition, then rate yourself.

The next time you see the word depends on your rating. Words you know well appear less often. Words you struggle with come back sooner. This is called spaced repetition — it's the same technique used by Anki.

## Rating a Card

After revealing the back, rate how well you remembered:

| Button | Key | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| Again | 1 | You forgot entirely |
| Hard | 2 | You recalled with difficulty |
| Good | 3 | You recalled correctly |
| Easy | 4 | You recalled instantly |

You can also tap the **left half** of the card for Again, or the **right half** for Good. A colored toast confirms your choice.

## Card Counts

The top of the screen shows three colored indicators:

- **Blue** — New cards you're seeing for the first time today
- **Red** — Cards you're relearning after forgetting
- **Green** — Cards coming back for a scheduled review

The daily limit for new cards is 20 by default. You can change it in [Mipangilio](/docs/general/settings).

## Context & Translation

Every card shows the sentence where you saved the word. The exact word form is highlighted. Below it you'll see where the word came from (the video title or text name, plus the date).

If you have "Onyesha tafsiri" enabled in Mipangilio, the card shows the Kiingereza translation of the sentence when you reveal the back. If no saved translation exists, it fetches one automatically.

The context sentence has a hover menu (the three dots) for copying the text, speaking it aloud, getting an AI explanation, or translating it on the spot.

## Removing a Word

To remove a word from your review deck, unsave it from the dictionary entry on the back of the card. The next card slides into place automatically.

## Keyboard Shortcuts

- **Space** or **Enter** — Reveal the back, or rate Good (when back is shown)
- **1–4** — Rate Again, Hard, Good, Easy (when back is shown)

## Tips

- Mapitio daily. Even 5 minutes makes a difference.
- Be strict with Again. If you couldn't recall it in real life, it counts as a fail.
- Use the context sentence. Seeing the word in its original sentence is more effective than memorizing in isolation.
- Tap the three dots on the context sentence to translate or explain the full line before rating.
- Adjust your daily new card limit in Mipangilio if 20 feels too many or too few.

title: Saved Words
content:
# Saved Words

Your personal vocabulary collection. Every word you bookmark from subtitles, the dictionary, or the popup ends up here.

## How Words Get Saved

You can save a word from anywhere:

- **In a video** — click a word in the subtitles, then hit the bookmark icon in the popup.
- **In the dictionary** — click the bookmark on any search result or entry page.
- **From the sidebar** — while browsing one entry, click a saved word in the sidebar to jump to it.

When you save a word, the app automatically fetches all its inflected and conjugated forms. This means when you encounter "went" later, it's recognized as the same word you saved as "go."

## Browsing Your Collection

The Saved Words page lists every word you've bookmarked for your current target language. You can:

- **Search** — type in the filter box to find words by their form, the sentence they appeared in, or the video title.
- **Sort** — switch between newest first or alphabetical order.
- **Scan by date** — words saved today are grouped separately from older ones.

Each word row shows the head form, its definition, the sentence context, and where it came from (video title, textbook, or dictionary).

Click any word to open its full dictionary entry.

## Export and Clear

- **Export** — download your entire vocabulary list as a JSON file. Useful for backups or importing into other tools.
- **Clear all** — remove every saved word for this language. This can't be undone.

## Review and Practice

Saved words feed into the [Review](/docs/vocab/review) system. The app uses spaced repetition to schedule each word for review just before you'd forget it. Words are shown as flashcards — see the word, recall the meaning, and rate how well you remembered.

A colored dot next to each word in your saved list shows its review status: blue for new, amber for due, red for overdue, green for reviewed.

## Tips

- **Save as you watch** — bookmark interesting words during a video, then review them later. You don't need to stop and study mid-video.
- **Use the filter** to find all words from a specific video or TV show. Type part of the video title in the search box.
- **Export regularly** as a backup. The JSON file includes every form, definition, and context for each word.
- **Click through to the entry** for any saved word to see its full conjugation tables, video examples, and AI explanation.
